perTask.note: core util, very common name -> grep-hostile (27 callers in-graph)

discrepancies.note: fetch.js:75 `env = utils.merge.call({skipUndefined:true}, {Request,Response}, env)` inside the `factory` arrow. Direct call to the target utils.merge. Both methods agree.
Axios.js:150 `let contextHeaders = headers && utils.merge(headers.common, headers[config.method])`. Falls inside `_request` (node line 82, loc 152 => lines 82-233). Direct call. Both agree.
mergeConfig.js:39 `utils.merge.call({caseless}, target, source)` and :41 `utils.merge({}, source)`, both inside the nested `getMergedValue` (lines 37-46). Innermost-correct direct caller. Both agree.

Over-attribution by codeweb's line-range containment: the two utils.merge calls physically sit inside nested `getMergedValue`, whose range (37-46) is fully contained in `mergeConfig` (17-159), so the call bleeds up to the enclosing function. mergeConfig's own lexical body does not directly write `utils.merge`. Kept as REAL because mergeConfig is the enclosing function of the call site AND a genuine transitive dependent (invokes getMergedValue which calls merge); per the task's 'or otherwise depend on it'. NOTE: the LOCAL `const merge` at mergeConfig.js:139-143 is a DIFFERENT binding (config-merge dispatcher = mergeMap[prop]|mergeDeepProperties), NOT the target, and correctly did not create an edge.

utils.js:477 `merge(existing, val)` and :479 `merge({}, val)` inside the nested `assignValue` helper (defined inside merge, lines 463-485). Recursive self-use via the nested closure; a real reference to the symbol. Both agree.

Module-scope reference: `merge` is listed in the default-export object at utils.js:983 (`export default { ... merge, ... }`). This is the export that makes the symbol reachable as utils.merge everywhere else. Real module-scope reference. Both agree.

Real references: utils.merge(...) called 9x (lines 68,76,83,90,106,122,137,156, and 1048/1059 in the prototype-chain-read describe). The graph DOES contain this edge but as kind:'test' (prototypePollution.test.js:<module> -> lib/utils.js:merge [test]). codeweb `--callers` filters to kind:'call' only, so it is OMITTED from --callers output (and from --impact). It WOULD appear under `--tests`/`--dependents`. Manual grep found it; codeweb's chosen query did not surface it.

The DEDICATED unit test for the target: imports `import utils from '../../../lib/utils.js'` then `const { merge } = utils;` (lines 2-4) and calls merge(...) ~30x plus merge.call(...). Unambiguously the target symbol. This file is NOT in the graph at all (155/repo files indexed; tests/unit/utils/ subdir tests excluded) -> zero nodes/edges, so codeweb cannot return it via ANY query. Pure corpus-grep find. Coverage gap in the pilot graph, not a query bug.

Excluded from confirmedTruth.
Matched only on the substring isAxiosError / `payload.isAxiosError === true` (L13) — a duck-typed property check, never the AxiosError class. Correctly excluded by both --callers and the graph.

The only AxiosError token in dispatchRequest.js is a CODE COMMENT at L53 ('...attach it to any AxiosError it throws'). Not a real reference. Correctly excluded from --callers (appears in --impact only transitively via transformResponse).

discrepancies.note: Both found it. Import at line 21 (module scope) + uses at 724 (new AxiosHeaders()), 735 (AxiosHeaders.from), 1146 (new AxiosHeaders(res.headers)). Use-sites are physically inside the inner `dispatchHttpRequest` (the wrapAsync executor body of `httpAdapter`); graph has no dispatchHttpRequest node so attributing to the enclosing `httpAdapter` is correct granularity.

Both found it. Self/definition module-scope references: line 329 AxiosHeaders.accessor([...]), 339 reduceDescriptors(AxiosHeaders.prototype,...), 349 freezeMethods(AxiosHeaders), 351 export default AxiosHeaders. Real references to the symbol (definition-site), so codeweb counting module scope here is consistent.

import AxiosHeaders (line 17) + `axios.AxiosHeaders = AxiosHeaders` (line 78) — the public re-export attaching the class to the default instance. Both at module scope. codeweb omitted this real reference. This is the single most important consumer (exposes the symbol publicly) yet it was dropped.
